children.text: support engagement and collaboration with private-sector industry and the community surrounding the location of such office; and
accelerate the use and adoption of commercially-developed technologies for national security purposes.

children.heading: .—Members
children.label: b .—Members
children.text: Each Secretary of a military department shall select for the Detachment, and make efforts to retain, members of the reserve components who possess relevant private-sector experience in the fields of business, acquisition, intelligence, engineering, technology transfer, science, mathematics, program management, logistics, cybersecurity, or such other fields as determined by the Secretary of Defense.

children.label: A
children.text: expertise on and analysis of commercially-developed technologies;

children.label: B
children.text: commercially-developed technologies to be used as alternatives for technologies in use by the Department; and

children.label: C
children.text: opportunities for greater engagement and collaboration between the Department and private-sector industry on innovative technologies.

children.label: i
children.text: identify and rapidly prototype commercially-developed technologies; and

children.label: ii
children.text: use alternative contracting mechanisms to procure such technologies;

children.label: i
children.text: the work of the Defense Innovation Unit; and

children.label: II
children.text: National Defense Science and Technology Strategy as directed under section 218 of the John S. McCain National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2019 (; ); andPublic Law 115–232132 Stat. 1679

children.label: III
children.text: relevant policy and guidance from the Secretary of Defense; and

children.label: C
children.text: using the investment in research and development made by private-sector industry in assessing and developing dual-use technologies.
Carrying out other activities as directed by the Secretary of Defense.

children.heading: .—Joint Duty
children.label: d .—Joint Duty
children.text: Assignment to a Detachment shall not qualify as a joint duty assignment, as defined in , United States Code, unless approved by the Secretary of Defense.section 668(b)(1) of title 10
